question_text: A block of wood floats on water, but a block of iron sinks. What does this tell us about their densities?
correct_answer: Wood has a lower density than water; iron has a higher density than water. Objects float if less dense than water and sink if more dense.
explanation: Objects float when their density is less than the liquid they're in, and sink when their density is greater. Wood is less dense than water so it floats, while iron is more dense than water so it sinks.
